channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Fallout Remaster Rumor Shut Down by Suspected Studio
item.content:encoded:
Over the last couple of days, Fallout fans have been puffing the hopium pipe, firm in the belief that the developer of the Fallout 3 and Fallout: New Vegas remaster projects had been uncovered.

Iron Galaxy Studios posted an image a few days back showing a company presentation with a classic Fallout cover image, prompting many to believe the company was working on the remasters. The rumor gained traction because of Iron Galaxy’s experience working closely with Bethesda Game Studios.

The team has just emerged to take the wind out of everyone’s sails.

Iron Galaxy Studios is NOT Working on Fallout

Iron Galaxy Studios is the latest company to be eliminated from the Fallout remaster pool. Following the posting of an image on social media that made everyone believe they were working on the Bethesda-backed remasters, the team has surfaced to shoot down the rumors.

In a post on social media, they wrote:

Nope! There is nothing to see here. Sorry to disturb the volcanoes, but that was just a BTS look at our company meeting. We use that slide every month and has nothing to do with anything Fallout being worked on. As you can imagine, we love Fallout, too. Now pardon us as we retreat back into our vault.

Of course, fans were quick to point out that that’s exactly what a company secretly working on the most highly anticipated remasters in years would say. The whole ‘retreat to the vault’ line is enough to make fans still believe that Iron Galaxy is connected in some way to the remasters.

At this point, we have no idea who is working on Fallout 3 and Fallout: New Vegas remastered, nor how far along they are in the development lifecycle. We know that multiple Fallout games are in the works, thanks to Todd Howard, but things are being kept buttoned up quite nicely.

Who do you think is piecing together the Fallout remasters? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Sir Ben Kingsley Lands Key Role in Sonic the Hedgehog 4
item.content:encoded:
A recent report indicates that Sir Ben Kingsley has been cast in a significant role in “Sonic the Hedgehog 4.”

According to Jeff Sneider’s report, the specifics of Kingsley’s role have not yet been disclosed. Still, if true, he is set to be joining a star-studded cast that includes Jim Carrey, Ben Schwartz, James Marsden, and others. Most recently, it was announced that Amy Rose will be voiced by Kristen Bell.

Previous Sonic films featured Idris Elba as the voice of Knuckles the Echidna, likely to return. Also, Keanu Reeves was featured as the voice of Shadow the Hedgehog in the third film.

What Could Be Next for the Sonic Movie Franchise

The third Sonic movie largely centered around the plot of Sonic Adventure 2 and the introduction of Shadow the Hedgehog. At the end of the film, there was a teaser that included Amy Rose. Sega first introduced Rose in Sonic CD, a Sega CD game released in 1993. The villain of that game was Metal Sonic.

A plot point in Sonic CD was time travel, which is what the films have done in a way by introducing Shadow before Amy. It is possible that the next film could draw on plot points from Sonic CD and mesh them with other plot points in Sonic’s history. However, a full film synopsis remains to be released.

Sonic the Hedgehog 4 is scheduled for release on March 19, 2027. Insider Gaming will continue to provide details on the film’s cast and plot as more information becomes available.

Recently, Sega announced plans to adapt other classic games into animated projects. Franchises teased include Streets of Rage and OutRun. Learn more about what to expect in the near future from Sega at this link.

Are you looking forward to the fourth Sonic the Hedgehog movie? Who do you think Kingsley will play in the movie? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Marathon’s Toughest Map (So Far) Only Has a 35% Survival Rate
item.content:encoded:
Bungie has unveiled a slew of stats for Marathon’s recent server slam, the global test held ahead of the game’s full launch on March 5. In a short video posted on social media, various facts and figures were revealed, including a tidbit stating that almost one million runs were carried out using a ‘Rook’, the ‘basic’ Runner Shell in Marathon with no strings attached.

One of the most interesting facts concerned Dire Marsh, the toughest map in the server slam, which was a little more threatening than Perimeter, the ‘entry-level’ map. It was revealed that Dire Marsh boasted a shocking survival rate of just 35.6%, which means that 64% of all players who entered the Marsh never came out again.

You Win Some, You Lose Some… And Then You Lose Some More

When the server slam kicked off, hundreds of thousands of prospective Marathon fans found themselves diving into Perimeter, the introductory map that served as little more than a glorified tutorial ground.

Things escalated when those same users headed to Dire Marsh, which, as the name suggests, is a little trickier to manage.

The proof of that concept is in the numbers, revealed earlier today by Bungie in a social video. It was confirmed that the exfil rate on Dire Marsh was just 35.6%, and it’s easy to see that it is not the most complex, threatening map yet to surface in Marathon. There are more maps on the way, set to arrive both with and just after release.

Many ventured into Marathon with the hope that it would be an easier extraction shooter. It boasts a more accessible model, offering arcade-style gunplay and a range of classes, all with powerful abilities, but that hasn’t made for an easy ride for some. It’s going to get harder, too – Dire Marsh gets a night mode with the game’s second season.

The numbers posted in the video also revealed that more than 9.1 million Runners were killed by other players, while 129 million UESC enemies (the AI folks) were gunned down by that same player base.

And, in case you were wondering, 384,866 drinkable cheeseburgers were extracted from raids and taken back to stashes across the game.

How much of a struggle did you have playing on Dire Marsh? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news about The Division 2’s new roadmap

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…
